title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Total member shares stood at $845.47 million as of December 31, 2026, representing a 1.2% increase from the previous year-end. The growth reflects steady accumulation across multiple deposit categories amid continued balance-sheet adjustments and stable member funding inflows. Despite minor fluctuations in specific segments, the overall funding base remains robust and diversified.

Share drafts remain the largest funding component at $253.06 million (29.9%), followed by share certificates totaling $254.83 million (30.1%). Regular shares account for $208.69 million (24.7%), while money market shares total $104.46 million (12.4%). IRA/KEOGH accounts represent $23.88 million (2.8%), and all other shares comprise a negligible 0.1% of the mix.

Most core deposit categories experienced modest growth from the previous year-end, particularly share drafts (+1.2%) and regular shares (+3.2%). Share certificates increased by 0.2%, while money market shares grew slightly at 0.2%. All other shares saw a notable increase of 6.9%, although these balances remain immaterial to the overall funding structure.

Overall, CAMPUS's deposit structure remains well diversified and predominantly composed of core member funding. While total shares increased during the period, the institution maintains a stable funding base and strong liquidity position. Management continues to focus on retaining core member relationships, maintaining deposit competitiveness, and supporting long-term funding stability.
